Tests the behavior of object subtraction and assignment operations in JavaScript.

# Purpose
This code defines an object with custom `valueOf` and `toString` methods, and it demonstrates the behavior of these methods when the object is used in arithmetic operations. The `valueOf` method returns the number `15`, which is used when the object is involved in arithmetic operations. The `toString` method returns an empty string, but it is not used in this code. The object is assigned to the variable `copyObject`, and an assertion checks that subtracting `2` from `object` results in the same value as subtracting `2` from `copyObject`, confirming that both variables reference the same object. This code provides narrow functionality, focusing on object method behavior and reference equality in JavaScript.
